channel.title: SevenAtSevenLastSection
channel.description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from SevenAtSevenLastSection, skipping any feeds with errors.

item.title: Book Riot’s Deals of the Day for February 20, 2026
item.description: A queer space opera on the wreckage of war, a genre-bending memoir, a modern gothic fantasy, and more of today's best book deals

item.title: The Most Read Books on Goodreads This Week
item.description: Here are the most read books on Goodreads this week! These are the buzziest books of the moment, from Wuthering Heights to The Housemaid.

item.title: Unsettle Down: 8 Creepy Post-Apocalyptic Novels
item.description: What post-apocalyptic novel gives you the creep?

item.title: Weekly Meal Plan #78
item.description: We’ve made it to week 78, which means these weekly meal plans are working! Thank you for letting me be…

item.title: The Disappearance of Young Adult Books and Services in Public Libraries: Book Censorship News, February 20, 2026
item.description: Teen collections in public libraries are disappearing. So, too, are professionals dedicated to serving this demographic.

item.title: In Prison, Optics Are Everything
item.description: Assumptions and rumors determine the social hierarchy and the wrong friendship can be dangerous.

item.title: Inside the Wuthering Heights Marketing Machine
item.description: An army of influencers, a bundle of brand partnerships, and a big box office take. Plus: a new Barbara Kingsolver novel, and more.

item.title: Federal Prisons Bar Gender-Affirming Care for Trans People
item.description: ‘People will die,’ an advocate warns, as standard treatments for gender dysphoria are replaced with therapy and antidepressants after Trump’s order.

item.title: Missouri Journalists: Data for Investigating Police Misconduct
item.description: Use The Marshall Project’s database of more than 600 police misconduct cases in Missouri to power your reporting.

item.title: How Often Does Child Welfare Call Police in Your State for Positive Drug Tests at Birth?
item.description: The Marshall Project found 70,000+ allegations of pregnancy drug use referred to law enforcement, often based on unreliable drug tests.
